item.title: Virginia Voters Ignored
item.description: FP Edit: Folks pay attention the time to fix what we can. Let me know your thoughts below. And a historic screw you to civil rights. Virginia Supreme Court denies voter-approved redistricting maps. This is a large attack on civil rights and is the start of more to come. People ignored. Roe v. Wade. A lot of people ignored the attack on the LGBTQIA and trans community. People still ignored the attack on the Spanish Latino communities. Apparently long forgotten have been the natives. The poem that speaks about the came for one group, and it was ignored, has almost run through the full course. You need to pay attention. https://youtube.com/shorts/PqI7SxpSNls (Would really appreciate it if you go watch the video here. Hit like and comment. It helps beyond measure.) https://linktr.ee/GridSquares
item.dc:creator: SquaresUnfiltered

item.title: TIL some birbs do dust baths
item.description: Cincinnati Zoo- “Toby the new African Pygmy falcon takes a dust bath. ? Toby is 9-months-old. Keepers are hopeful he will become a companion to Tanzi, the female Pygmy Falcon who stars in the summer bird experience!” (Sound on for dusty flapping, no cheesy music)

item.title: NEVER STOP TALKING ABOUT THE EPSTEIN FILES... it's not just about Trump, it's about a billionaire elite order of pedophiles leveraging governments, academia, banking, entertainment, law & order to manipulate reality away from 8 billion of us. PLEASE VOTE!
item.description: There's about 12 things the Dems can do immediately if they get back in power to stabilize and fix the American government and United States... Short term, impeach Trump. Jack Smith's charges were dismissed *without prejudice*... we can still try him and hold him accountable. The other thing about 2028 if 2026 isn't ripped from democracy... House and Senate are broken on purpose... that means Trump's entire admin (not the lasting harm, but the actions) can be undone by a stroke of the pen. Trump's presidency is all Executive Order, and can be unraveled overnight. The long term effects however... be civil and civic minded in understanding we're going to need kind, smart, trustworthy, and talented people to rebuild the institutions of this country. Other longer term things we can fix: Hardest? Reverse Citizens United (needs constitutional amendment). But so close to substantive change for betterment of all Americans. A majority gets: Overturn filibuster John Lewis Voting Rights Act Expand SCOTUS SCOTUS Code of Ethics & Term Limits Term Limits SCOTUS Term Limits Senate and House Age limit changes? DISCLOSE ACT (dark money) Presidential ethics requirements Make old blown out norms back into laws and many more! That's all low hanging fruit, in some sense.

item.description:
Forbes says he worth well over 800Bil btw https://www.forbes.com/profile/elon-musk/
10s of thousands of Americans die every year from not being able to afford healthcare. 9 million people starve to death every year, 3 million of them being children. If you don’t understand how this is connected to an economic system that concentrates wealth in the hands of the most evil and greedy among us then it’s time to wake up.
item.dc:creator: Mgxr44

item.title: Or if your political ideology is too left wing
item.description: Since some people have pointed this out: the paragraphs quoted are real (will put the link below), but come from two different parts of the document. The paragraph that begins "Our new US Counterterrorism Strategy" is taken from the last part of the forward. The rest is from page 7, including part of a paragraph that begins on page 6. Others have posted the link in the comments, but will include it below as well. Whoever put this together probably should have put a line to indicate these are separate excerpts, but to be fair I don't think it's that misleading. The excerpts were taken directly from a WH document. Together, these provide a concise summary of their intent to violently suppress pro-transgender and 'anarchist' groups and individuals. It is not ambiguous. The forward says 'we will find you and kill you', the rest of the document summarizes who they are intending to find and kill. https://www.whitehouse.gov/wp-content/uploads/2026/05/2026-USCT-Strategy-1.pdf
item.dc:creator: Freyja33

item.title: Return of the Meme: 1683 (recreational plutonium)
item.description:
Let's get this bread. (Iranian flatbread)
This is a label I made for a Blood Orange and Sage mead.
Someone has been posting on this subreddit birding memes from China, and they are amazing.
Eurasian Blackbirds, like crows, can recognize specific people.
It's interesting to re-read books that I read when I was a teen or in my 20s.
Wood thrushes definitely have a bit of a pompous air to them.
They get angry eyebrows in spring and they look hilariously cute and angry.
Art by Natee Himmapan
Pippin tax Today's message from Pippin: "Peekaboo Peekaboo peekaboo peekaboo glory Pippin." ______ 1) Are you on the run to a safe place and passing through Arizona on your way to safer grounds? My apartment is open to you to crash at, and it is a safe space. Pippin and I will make you a meal and gladly share tea, wine, or mead with you. 2) Hey! Take your meds with a full glass of water, please. Do it for Pippin, or Space Mom, or better yet, do it for yourself. 3) Ĝefeloað wē. We will carry on. 4) all funds from the sales of items at these two links will be sent to support charities aiding to fight for Trans rights in the US (not just T-shirts!) https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74812801-all-conures-are-anarchists https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74811778-whiptail-pride 5) Since multiple people have requested this over the last two weeks or so, my ko-fi is: https://ko-fi.com/codyandpippin
item.dc:creator: CodyBurkett

item.title: GovBrief Today #465
item.description: Some drivers in downtown Chicago paid $5.78 a gallon for regular today. That price is the cost of 72 days of war Trump initially told the New York Times would take "four to five weeks." The 60-day War Powers Resolution clock expired May 1, and Hegseth told Congress the ceasefire pauses it, even though drones hit four countries this weekend. The Energy Secretary's response to a 50% gas price jump was to float a suspension of the 18-cent federal gas tax. Eighteen cents will not touch what 72 days of war is doing to Americans at the pump. Trump's Republican enablers in the Senate keep voting down war powers resolutions, but the votes are getting closer. The last one failed by only three. Use 5 Calls or Resistbot to push your senators to vote yes on the next one. I've emailed 7,337 stories over 465 straight nights, free and without ads. The algorithm can't show them all to you. Add your name at govbrief.today and I will. #GovBriefToday #Resist Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com Don't miss easy-click links, more context, data, and pictures on the website: https://go.govbrief.today/260510-full [1] https://go.govbrief.today/iran-war-may10 [2] https://go.govbrief.today/moms-gov-web [3] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-election-army [4] https://go.govbrief.today/hegseth-kelly [5] https://go.govbrief.today/fed-gas-tax [6] https://go.govbrief.today/buy-american Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.dc:creator: Pasquinelthe2nd

item.title: Residents Furious After Their Town Board Rejected an OpenAI Data Center, But a Billionaire Developer Forced It Through Anyway ... the Billionaire Who Claims 'Tax the Rich' is Hate Speech ...
item.description: Across the United States, the fight over data center construction has largely pitted stamp-happy municipal governments against their own residents. As these battles multiply, local officials are increasingly pressured to approve projects their constituents oppose, while more and more voters take their struggles to the ballot box. Residents in Saline Township, Michigan, however, thought they had avoided the drama after their township board and planning commission both voted to decline a 21 million square foot data center in their backyard. It was exactly what Saline’s 2,883-some residents wanted. Unfortunately for them, the data center developer soon sued the tiny township, Fortune reported, which was ultimately bullied into accepting the $16 billion development. Back in September, Saline’s planning commission rejected the request to rezone 575 acres of farmland for the data center, proposed by the company Related Digital, a subsidiary of a real estate conglomerate owned by billionaire Steven Roth (who’s been in the news lately for other reasons.) Two days later, Related Digital filed suit, alleging the township had practice “exclusionary zoning.” The local officials were up against the wall: a lengthy legal battle risked depleting the township’s coffers, and even if they won in court — an unlikely proposition — Related Digital could have forced the data center anyway by partnering with the University of Michigan, which can bypass local zoning laws. The township soon settled, signing an agreement allowing the project to proceed. Later in October, it was revealed that the data center would be primarily leased to Sam Altman’s OpenAI and Larry Ellison’s Oracle, as part of Donald Trump’s $500 billion AI infrastructure initiative, dubbed “Stargate.” “I’m not sure there were any good solutions,” Fred Lucas, the township’s attorney told Fortune. “If you polled everyone on the township board, they would have said the same thing: they didn’t want a data center there. We didn’t invite them, we didn’t encourage them.” The legal strong-arming in Saline exposes a fundamental contradiction in America’s AI infrastructure boom: it’s being imposed from above by tech billionaires and their political allies, not chosen by the communities forced to live with it. Town after town has been compelled to absorb the environmental and social costs of an industry obsessed with expansion — making it clear that when capital and democracy collide, capital wins. Kathryn Haushalter, a local mother who lives near the data center site told Fortune that “it feels like I’m playing by a different rule book. Like I’m playing baseball and they’re playing football.” https://futurism.com/artificial-intelligence/data-center-openai-residents Real estate baron Steve Roth, who recently declared that the phrase “tax the rich” offends him just as much as the most noxious racial slur. https://futurism.com/future-society/billionaire-rich-taxes-slurs
item.dc:creator: Jbelkin
